questions.question: Why is `shallowRef` recommended for widget data in a dashboard architecture?
options: It prevents other widgets from accidentally reading the same data
Widget data is typically replaced wholesale from an API response, so deep reactivity overhead is unnecessary
It is required when using `useFetch` with dynamic URLs from a Pinia store
Shallow refs automatically serialize to JSON for localStorage persistence

questions.question: Where should data shared between multiple dashboard widgets (e.g., a date range filter) be stored?
options: In the `DashboardPage` component's local `ref` and passed down as props
In a Pinia store so all dependent widgets react automatically when it changes
In the URL query string so widgets read it on mount
In a composable that each widget instantiates independently

questions.question: What is the benefit of each widget handling its own loading and error state independently?
options: It allows the page to use a single `<Suspense>` boundary for all widgets simultaneously
One slow or failing API does not block or break the other widgets on the page
It reduces the number of reactive refs needed in the parent component
It ensures all widgets finish loading at the same time for visual consistency
